issues: The headline in the draft ('Moncton Hawks Rally Past Baltimore Skipjacks 6-4') does not appear verbatim in the OCR; the OCR does not show a clear headline for this article.
The draft attributes two goals to Matt Hervey ('Hervey scored twice'), but the OCR only explicitly states Hervey cut the lead to 3-2 at 2:33 of the third period — no second Hervey goal is mentioned.

The draft states 'Jamie Gilhen added two goals' but the OCR attributes the goals to 'Jamie Husgen' scoring at 5:48 and 'Randy Gilhen' (not Jamie Gilhen) scoring at 13:36 and 15:43 — these appear to be two different players conflated into one.
The draft says 'five-goal third-period comeback' which is supported by the OCR, but misattributes the goals to the wrong players.
The byline field is left empty which appears correct based on the OCR.
review.correctedDescriptionShort: Moncton Hawks defeat Baltimore Skipjacks 6-4 in AHL play, with ex-Sockeyes defenseman Matt Hervey converted to right wing and contributing to a five-goal third-period rally.
review.correctedDescriptionLong: The Moncton Hawks moved into third place in the AHL's Northern Division by defeating the Baltimore Skipjacks 6-4 on October 28, 1987, before 1,799 fans at Baltimore Arena. Coach Rick Bowness converted defensemen Matt Hervey (ex-Richmond Sockeyes) and Jamie Husgen to right wing, and their hard-hitting play sparked a five-goal third-period comeback after the Hawks trailed 4-2. Randy Gilhen scored twice in the third, including the winner at 15:43, with Chris Levasseur adding an insurance goal; Hervey scored once to cut the early deficit.
review.notes: Matt Hervey is a former 1987 Sockeyes player now with the Moncton Hawks (AHL), making this archive-relevant. The draft conflates Randy Gilhen and Jamie Husgen into a fictional 'Jamie Gilhen,' and incorrectly credits Hervey with two goals. OCR does not show a clear standalone headline for this article. Byline not visible in OCR.
